title: Onoseris donnell-smithii
taxonomy: Onoseris donnell-smithii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Onoseris, which is a group of plants native to Mexico and Central America. The species name donnell-smithii indicates that the plant was first described by William R. Maxon in honor of Donnell Smith. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its appearance and growth habits. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification and any potential reclassifications.

intro: Onoseris donnell-smithii, also known as Mexican Onoseris, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Onoseris donnell-smithii include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Onoseris donnell-smithii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
